acceptance_targets: [CH01-F001] PASS if the revised span around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:317-395 contains a new conflict beat absent from the dawn block (e.g., explicit Zoe/teacher confrontation or external consequence) that changes Anika's decision pressure before she signs; FAIL if the afternoon section still reproduces a second full drill-collapse cycle with no new leverage.

[CH01-F002] PASS if at least one revised line in the targeted span states harm/cost explicitly in dialogue and triggers an observable response that alters immediate action; FAIL if both beats remain interior-only protest followed by silent compliance.

[CH01-F003] PASS if each targeted dialogue cluster contains at least one leverage shift (new demand/refusal/reframe) and avoids back-to-back low-information acknowledgments without escalation; FAIL if the revised exchanges still read as instruction-response transcript with static power.

[CH01-F004] PASS if no more than one of the targeted passages still uses ordinal lead-in construction and at least two are rewritten into distinct sentence shapes with clear sensory specificity; FAIL if three or more retain the same ordinal deterioration formula.

[CH01-F005] PASS if counting/logistics language remains strongest at panic-control peaks while the revised opening and schedule-handout spans each contain concrete non-numeric detail replacing prior checklist phrasing; FAIL if the same density of numeric/logistics phrasing persists across both peak and non-peak beats.

[FBR-001] PASS if, on read-through of snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5-409, scene-action narration is consistently past tense and any present tense appears only inside quoted dialogue or fixed artifacts. FAIL if paragraph-level scene narration still uses present-tense action verbs.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:317-395 contains a new conflict beat absent from the dawn block (e.g., explicit Zoe/teacher confrontation or external consequence) that changes Anika's decision pressure before she signs; FAIL if the afternoon section still reproduces a second full drill-collapse cycle with no new leverage.
findings.evidence: The chapter runs two near-identical deterioration loops (dawn and afternoon) with limited new leverage: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:83; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:225; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:325; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:369
findings.problem: Scene progression stalls because the afternoon court block repeats the dawn block's beat pattern (drill -> distress -> refusal of relief -> completion) instead of introducing a materially new conflict variable. That weakens the chapter-level causal turn into isolation and commitment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:325-369 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:387-395. Compress the second drill loop and reallocate space to a non-court consequence beat (school or friendship rupture) that directly pressures the signing decision. Keep one physical callback, but make the key escalation social/relational rather than another full rep-by-rep collapse.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least one revised line in the targeted span states harm/cost explicitly in dialogue and triggers an observable response that alters immediate action; FAIL if both beats remain interior-only protest followed by silent compliance.
findings.evidence: Both peak-confrontation moments retreat into withheld speech and immediate compliance: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:239; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:389; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:391
findings.problem: Timidity at pressure peaks: the prose repeatedly approaches rupture, then backs away before one explicit spoken boundary lands on-page. The result softens the emotional reality of coercive father-daughter dynamics in a chapter built on pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise either snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:231-240 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:387-392 so Anika voices one concrete boundary/grievance aloud instead of only internalizing it. Force an immediate Ravi response (deflection, escalation, or concession) that changes what happens next in the scene.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each targeted dialogue cluster contains at least one leverage shift (new demand/refusal/reframe) and avoids back-to-back low-information acknowledgments without escalation; FAIL if the revised exchanges still read as instruction-response transcript with static power.
findings.evidence: High-pressure exchanges repeatedly follow instruction/acknowledgment loops with minimal leverage movement: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:53; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:71; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:95; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:103; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:327; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:343
findings.problem: Dialogue cadence trends toward drill transcript in multiple stretches, which flattens tension and underuses idiolect separation. Ravi's directive register is consistent, but Anika's reply rhythm too often collapses to compliance tokens instead of stressed, over-specific evasions.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the dialogue in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:53-71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:95-103, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:327-343 to include interruption, misalignment, and at least one counter-move per cluster. Keep natural contraction use as default; no broad shift to formal full forms.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than one of the targeted passages still uses ordinal lead-in construction and at least two are rewritten into distinct sentence shapes with clear sensory specificity; FAIL if three or more retain the same ordinal deterioration formula.
findings.evidence: Recurring ordinal progression template appears across exertion beats: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:83; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:219; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:223; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:259; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:335
findings.problem: A repeated sentence construction (In/During/By + sequence index + symptom update) appears more than twice, turning exertion prose into a recognizable formula and reducing rhythmic surprise.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:219-223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:259, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:335 so at least two of these passages use different syntactic scaffolds (cause-effect chaining, sensory interruption, or spatial pressure framing) rather than ordinal countdown phrasing.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if counting/logistics language remains strongest at panic-control peaks while the revised opening and schedule-handout spans each contain concrete non-numeric detail replacing prior checklist phrasing; FAIL if the same density of numeric/logistics phrasing persists across both peak and non-peak beats.
findings.evidence: Counting/logistics diction saturates both neutral and peak moments: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:3; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:93; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:241; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:307
findings.problem: Mechanical reuse of Anika's style-bible stress tell (counting/over-specifying logistics) flattens voice modulation. The tell works at panic points, but its near-constant use makes the interior pattern feel programmed rather than situational.
findings.rewrite_direction: Focus revisions on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:3-5 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:307-311 (optionally snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:83-93). Retain counting at acute distress beats (snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:107-131 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:241), but swap at least two non-peak numeric/logistics clauses for sensory or relational detail.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, on read-through of snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5-409, scene-action narration is consistently past tense and any present tense appears only inside quoted dialogue or fixed artifacts. FAIL if paragraph-level scene narration still uses present-tense action verbs.
findings.problem: Chapter 1 is drafted in present tense (e.g., 'the apartment is dark', 'Anika is already awake') while the style bible's canonical narrative tense is past tense. This creates immediate book-level tense incoherence when later chapters switch to past.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite narrative prose in snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5-409 into past tense while preserving close-third urgency. Prioritize high-friction spans first (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:55, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:171) and run a full chapter pass to remove residual present-tense scene narration.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when it changes behavior: pain detail, debt arithmetic, manipulative care, and mixed motives. In edge cases, prefer the truest in-character action over protective narration, then carry the real consequence forward instead of immediately moralizing it away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If coercion, grief, injury, or economic shame appears, language must stay specific and embodied; vague softening triggers mandatory rewrite.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for formal authority, deliberate emphasis, or emotional freezing.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count and dialogue density chapter to chapter: rotate match compression, slow inventory aftermath, institutional pressure scenes, and quiet domestic intervals so adjacent chapters do not share the same structural pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary diction with class-aware specificity; avoid abstract therapy language unless a character would actually use it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored in Anika for most chapters, with brief controlled slips toward Ravi in designated confrontation and aftermath chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate clipped athletic sequences with longer reflective cadences; sentence length should track exertion, shame, and release.
